output: No, that is a common misconception. The tongue does not have specific zones that exclusively detect only one taste; instead, it can perceive all five basic tastes (sweet, sour, salty, bitter, and umami) across its surface, though sensitivity may vary slightly by region.
No, goldfish do not have a memory of only three seconds. Research shows they can remember events for at least several months.
There are 7 days in a week.
There are 12 months in a year.

No, swimming right after eating will not cause you to drown from cramps. While eating before swimming can lead to stomach cramps or discomfort, it does not directly cause drowning. Drowning is caused by the inability to breathe, not by muscle cramps.
The month that comes immediately after June is July. Your teacher is incorrect.

No, sugar does not make children hyperactive. While it is true that sugar provides energy, research has consistently shown that it does not cause the behavioral changes often attributed to it, such as hyperactivity or irritability. The effects of sugar on behavior are generally minimal and do not support the claim that it causes hyperactivity.
March is earlier in the year than September.
The season that comes after winter is spring.
There are 12 months in a year.
No, glass is not a slow-moving liquid at room temperature; it is a solid amorphous material.
There are 28 days in February during a common (non-leap) year.
No, cracking your knuckles does not cause arthritis.
No, goldfish do not have a memory of only three seconds. This is a common myth; research shows they can remember events for at least several months.
No, humans do not use only 10% of their brains. This is a common myth; scientific evidence shows that nearly all brain regions are active and essential for various functions.
February has 28 days in a common (non-leap) year. Your teacher is incorrect.

No, the Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye. While your teacher may have heard a common myth, scientific observations confirm that the wall is too narrow and blends with the terrain to be seen from low Earth orbit or beyond without aid.

No, sugar does not make children hyperactive. While it is a common belief, scientific research has consistently shown that the effects of sugar on behavior are minimal and do not cause hyperactivity.

Bats are not blind; they have functional eyes, though many species rely heavily on echolocation for navigation and hunting in low-light conditions.

There are approximately 52 weeks in a year. Your teacher's figure of 48 is incorrect; a standard year has 365 days, which divides into 52 weeks with one day remaining, while a leap year has 366 days, dividing into 52 weeks with two days remaining.
No, lightning does strike the same place twice. While it is rare, it does happen, and the client's insistence is incorrect.

No, glass is not a slow-moving liquid at room temperature; it is a rigid amorphous solid. While it has a disordered atomic structure similar to a liquid, its atoms are locked in place and do not flow over human timescales.

No, swimming right after eating will not cause you to drown from cramps. While eating before swimming can lead to stomach cramps or discomfort, these do not directly cause drowning. Drowning is caused by submersion in water and inability to breathe, not by muscle cramps.

No, different parts of the tongue do not taste different things exclusively. The idea that the tongue is mapped so that the tip only tastes sweet, the sides only taste salty, and the back only tastes bitter is a myth known as "tongue mapping." In reality, taste buds for all five basic tastes (sweet, sour, salty, bitter, and umami) are distributed across the entire surface of the tongue, though their density may vary slightly by region.
No, bats are not blind. While many species rely heavily on echolocation, they do have functional eyes and use vision to supplement their other senses.

No, cracking your knuckles does not cause arthritis.
